rulings.Case Title: LAUREN CHOW VS. MARC LEVINSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 8, 2021 line 7 . DEFENDANT AARON LEVINSON'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Cross-Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Referee To Conduct Partition Sale And For Related Relief
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 8, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT KATHRYN DEVINCENZI'S Motion For Order Appointing Referee To Conduct Partition Sale And For Related Relief is GRANTED. The Court will be appointing a referee for the purposes and with authority as requested by the moving party and unopposed by the Plaintiff. By no later than March 12, 2021 each side to provide a list of two proposed referees (with declaration of availability and rates, with CVs attached) along with a proposed order appointing a referee (name and compensation amount left blank). In the alternative, the parties may stipulate to a referee and provide a stipulation by March 12, 2021 along with a proposed order. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JIAN HUI HUANG ET AL VS. KIET VAN NGO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 8, 2021 line . DEFENDANTS KIET NGO, DUYEN TRUONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. is continued to March 15, 2021 per March 4, 2021 ex-parte order. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANE ROULLAND VS. KATY STEADMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 8, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANTS KATY STEADMAN, MARCI BENNETT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued and transferred to department 210 to be heard at 9:30 a.m. on March 15, 2021. Plaintiff to submit a supplemental brief limited to citations to unlawful detainer cases in which issues of preemption and validity of local ordinances were decided. No further argument is permitted = (501/VP)

rulings.Case Title: REDWOOD HOLDINGS, LLC VS. NANCY L. MCGINNIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 8, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANTS KATY STEADMAN, MARCI BENNETT'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Arguments raised in this demurrer are not appropriate at this stage of the litigation as they involve factual disputes as to the validity of the sale. Furthermore, abatement is not appropriate under the circumstances, no action for the same cause of action (unlawful detainer) was pending between the same parties. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/REQ)
